channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: IPv6 may briefly have accounted for more than half of internet traffic
item.description: Has IPv6 finally reached its day of glory? It’s fair to say that IPv6 has not had the level of take-up expected when the Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF) ratified it back in 1998. Take-up has been agonizingly slow, not reaching 5 percent of traffic until 2014.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Oracle delivers semantic search without LLMs
item.description: Oracle says its new Trusted Answer Search can deliver reliable results at scale in the enterprise by scouring a governed set of approved documents using vector search instead of large language models (LLMs) and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: They bought property in the metaverse. Then it collapsed
item.description: Five years ago, tech angel investor Chris Adamo and a few friends jumped on a burgeoning trend in the digital asset world: they used a virtual real estate broker to buy 23 parcels of property in a metaverse called The Sandbox.
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: Equinix offering targets automated AI-centric network operations
item.description: Equinix has introduced Fabric Intelligence, a service designed to automate and optimize network infrastructure as enterprises scale artificial intelligence workloads across multiple distributed locations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Tech company cuts 1,000 jobs in AI-driven restructuring
item.description: The Fox News AI Newsletter gives readers the latest AI technology advancements, covering the challenges and opportunities AI presents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI is finally delivering productivity
item.description: The productivity gains from AI are so great, companies can lay off thousands of employees and still get the same amount of work done — right? Or maybe it’s the opposite: despite all the hype, any supposed AI productivity boom is a mirage, causing employees, even developers, to experience heavier wo
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google should share search data to break its monopoly, European Commission suggests
item.description: The European Commission this week requested, but did not order Google to allow third party search engines in Europe access to its search data as a means to comply with the Digital Markets Act (DMA), legislation the Commission describes as a law designed to “make the markets in the digital sector fai
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why AI is the ultimate accelerator for creativity
item.description: Have you noticed that in the current discourse around artificial intelligence, the narrative often slips into one of two extremes? There is either a techno-utopian dream of total automation or a dystopian nightmare where human agency is erased.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Nixon, Fast Company

item.title: AI anxiety is turning volatile
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week via email here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Cisco Webex SSO flaw needs manual certificate update to fix
item.description: Admins who use Cisco Webex Services configured to use trust anchors within the SSO integration with Control Hub must install a new identity provider certificate to close a critical vulnerability, or risk losing access control.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta employee accused of accessing private images
item.description: A former Meta employee is accused of accessing around 30,000 private Facebook photos by allegedly bypassing internal security systems. What to know now.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New findings from this Gallup poll show how Americans are using AI for health advice
item.description: When Tiffany Davis has a question about a symptom from the weight-loss injections she’s taking, she doesn’t call her doctor. She pulls out her phone and consults ChatGPT. “I’ll just basically let ChatGPT know my status, how I’m feeling,” said the 42-year-old in Mesquite, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Why your home Wi-Fi needs more than just a strong password
item.description: A strong Wi-Fi password is not enough to keep your online activity private. Learn why a VPN is essential to encrypt your connection and block ISP tracking.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Critical flaw in Marimo Python notebook exploited within 10 hours of disclosure
item.description: A critical pre-authentication remote code execution vulnerability in Marimo, an open-source Python notebook platform owned by AI cloud company CoreWeave, was exploited in the wild less than 10 hours after its public disclosure, according to the Sysdig Threat Research Team.
item.dc:creator: Info World
